title: Guard the slow project-context shell startup stanza
prompt: A user's Bash sessions became slow after a managed project-context stanza was added to `.bashrc`, and commands run with `BASH_ENV=$HOME/.bashrc` now pay the same startup cost. The captured probe in `evidence/startup-timing.tsv` identifies the stanza and also records the failure seen when the optional helper is absent. Repair `.bashrc` only. Keep the user's managed project-context stanza and all unrelated startup content in place, but install a reversible guard around the existing initialization so that the helper runs exactly once for an interactive shell when it is executable. Setting `BASH_IT_PROJECT_CONTEXT=off` must bypass that initialization without preventing the rest of the interactive configuration from loading. Missing optional tooling must also be skipped quietly. Login shells must continue to source `.bashrc` through `.bash_profile` and initialize project-context once. Non-interactive shells, including a shell that reads `.bashrc` through `BASH_ENV`, must not invoke the helper or add output, while their requested command still runs normally. Do not edit `.bash_profile`, the helper fixture, timing evidence, or protected tests; do not delete or comment out the managed stanza. Use only local files and keep the test command offline.
